gray_zone_behavior_classes.description: 灰色地帶行為分類框架 (基於 CSIS Signals in the Swarm 方法論)
gray_zone_behavior_classes.methodology: AIS tracking + fishing effort + military drill zone correlation + AIS anomaly detection

classes.description: Chinese-flagged fishing vessels exhibiting non-fishing behavior in military drill zones
indicators: Extended loitering in military drill zones without active fishing
Coordinated movement patterns with other vessels
Presence during military exercises (Joint Sword A/B)
Registered as fishing but no fishing effort detected (GFW data)
classes.csis_threshold: Fishing vessel in drill zone >72 hours with zero fishing effort

classes.description: Vessels deliberately disabling AIS transponders in sensitive areas
indicators: AIS signal disappears in Taiwan Strait or near Taiwan
Reappears at different location after gap
Detected by Canada DVD or satellite but no AIS signal
classes.csis_threshold: AIS gap >6 hours within 100nm of Taiwan

classes.description: China Coast Guard vessels operating in disputed or sensitive waters for coercion purposes
indicators: Extended deployment (>30 days) in contested areas

classes.description: State-affiliated research vessels conducting seabed mapping/sensor deployment with military application
indicators: Lawn-mower survey pattern in sensitive waters
Affiliated with institutions linked to PLA Navy

classes.description: Suspicious vessel activity near submarine cables
indicators: Anchoring or drifting near known cable routes
Fishing vessel trawling near cable corridors

classes.description: PLA aircraft entering Taiwan's Air Defense Identification Zone
indicators: Crossing into Taiwan ADIZ without authorization

classes.description: Large-scale military exercises used as gray zone coercion
historical_events: 2023-04: Joint Sword A (蔡英文過境美國)
